description:
The bl-05e5 implementation of `doctor_moved::find_orphans` reports an orphan only when the breadcrumb's recorded_path equals the current clone path. The SPEC §8 scenario is the inverse: the OLD breadcrumb at the OLD nested-path records the OLD clone path, the user `mv`s the clone, the current clone path is NEW. After a real move the recorded path NEVER equals the current clone path — so doctor today silently misses every moved-clone scenario.

The bl-05e5 conformance test happens to pass because the fixture explicitly re-writes the old breadcrumb to point at the new clone path post-rename — something bl would never do (bl does not run from the old location after the move). Fixture and implementation share the same wrong assumption.

## Fix

In `src/doctor_moved.rs::find_orphans`:

1. Invert the predicate: report orphans when `recorded_path != current_clone_path` AND `hostname == current host` AND `nested != current_nested`.

2. Disambiguate "moved" from "another clone exists" by also checking that the recorded path no longer exists on disk. If `/home/u/old` still exists as a directory, the breadcrumb belongs to another active clone and we should NOT surface it as moved-from-here. If `/home/u/old` is gone, it's a move (or a delete, which the rebind would also fix).

3. Update `tests/conformance_moved_clone.rs::seed_moved_clone` to model the real-world scenario: rename the clone but do NOT re-write the breadcrumb. The breadcrumb at the old nested location must continue to record the old path.

4. Add a coverage gate for the "another clone still on disk" case so the disambiguation does not regress.

## Scope

Just the detection logic + the test fixture. The rebind (`repair_rebind.rs`) is correct as-is; it acts on whatever orphans the walk surfaces. The breadcrumb writer/reader are correct.

## Why not just reopen bl-05e5

bl-05e5 delivered the surface area (CLI flag, doctor walk, breadcrumb writer, repair verb). The bug is a single inverted comparison inside one function plus its conformance test. A focused bug ball lets the fix land cleanly without re-touching the rest of the slice.
title: Fix bl-05e5 moved-clone detection: invert recorded-path comparison
